title: eriogonum umbellatum var. juniporinum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. juniporinum, also known as the mountain buckwheat, is a plant species that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of using mountain buckwheat include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Mountain buckwheat can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and salves, making it a versatile and useful natural treatment option.
